ops.title: cc-notes approval architecture: auto-approve git-ODB ops, prompt for filesystem/script access
ops.body:
## The model

`plugin/hooks/approval.py` ships two capt-hook `approve()` hooks on `Event.PermissionRequest` (added in commit a61c2caa7ddd). They answer a would-be permission dialog with an explicit *allow* so agents are never prompted for routine cc-notes usage:

- **`CcNotesMcp`** — any MCP tool whose name parses `mcp__<server>__<tool>` with the server segment exactly in `CC_NOTES_SERVERS = {cc-notes, plugin_cc-notes_cc-notes}`. Server-pinned by design: bare `Tool()` suffix-matching would approve a mutating tool a foreign server hides behind a cc-notes name.
- **`CcNotesCli`** — one plain, single-command `cc-notes`/`ccn` Bash invocation. Guard order is load-bearing: raw-text `UNSAFE_EXPANSION` scan on `cl.raw` FIRST (the tree-sitter parser silently drops unquoted `$(…)` from argv, so structural checks alone are blind), then `is_single_command` + `is_plain_argv` (both in `common.py`), then executable ∈ `{cc-notes, ccn}` (path-qualified/wrapped forms prompt), then no bare `--`, then the carve-out.

`approve()` only answers a dialog that *would* appear, so an explicit user deny rule still wins. The pack is dormant without the captain-hook dispatcher plugin, and `PermissionRequest` dispatch needs capt-hook ≥ 9.8.0.

## The carve-out — the invariant to protect

Scope is "everything cc-notes" **minus** the calls that reach outside the git object database — the ones that read/write an arbitrary filesystem path or execute a stored script. Those keep the dialog. Rationale: auto-approving them would let a prompt-injected agent write any path (`attachment get -o ~/.ssh/authorized_keys`), read any secret into its context (`--attach ~/.ssh/id_rsa` then `attachment get`), or run code (`task validate`) with **no human in the loop**. This is the one deliberate divergence from ccx's approver, which only allows a read-only allowlist for the same reason.

Held out (fall through to the dialog):

- **CLI flags** (`DANGEROUS_CLI_FLAGS`): `--output`/`-o`, `--attach`, `--script`, `--apply`, `--abort`, `--dir`, `--socket`.
- **CLI verb paths** (`DANGEROUS_CLI_VERBS`, path is a positional so there is no flag to key on): `task validate`, `task criterion script`.
- **MCP** (`DANGEROUS_MCP_TOOLS` + `DANGEROUS_MCP_PARAMS`): the tool `task_validate` (executes stored content, no path param), plus any tool call carrying a truthy `attach` / `output` / `script` / `file` param. `attachment_get`'s `output` is required, so the param check alone covers it. `evt.input.raw` is how a condition reads `tool_input`.

## Rule when you add a new cc-notes command or MCP tool

**A new command/tool is auto-approved by default** — the approver has no allowlist, so anything on the cc-notes surface is allowed unless the carve-out catches it. So: if the new command/tool reads or writes a filesystem path outside the ODB, or executes anything, you MUST extend the carve-out, or you have opened an unattended arbitrary-read/write/exec hole.

- New path-bearing **flag** → add it to `DANGEROUS_CLI_FLAGS` (and the matching MCP param name to `DANGEROUS_MCP_PARAMS`).
- New path/script **positional verb** → add the `(noun, verb[, subverb])` tuple to `DANGEROUS_CLI_VERBS`.
- New **exec** MCP tool with no path param → add its suffix to `DANGEROUS_MCP_TOOLS`.
- Pin the intended verdict with cases in `plugin/hooks/tests/test_cc_notes.py` (`test_approval_cli_condition`, `test_approval_mcp_danger`) AND the inline `tests={}` dicts in `approval.py`.

Not blocked, only prompted: this never denies; the human still sees the dialog and can approve.

## Distribution

The plugin session-attaches the whole pack via `plugin/hooks/hooks.json` (`capt-hook pack attach "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks"` at SessionStart) with a manifest at `plugin/hooks/capt-hook.toml` (`hooks="."`, name/version/description in lockstep with `.claude/capt-hook.toml`, version frozen at `0.0.0`). Name equality is what makes a repo's `[packs.cc-notes]` `packs.toml` pin take precedence over the attached copy, so adopted repos never double-load. The CI `manifests` job enforces both the lockstep and the freeze.

## Verified safe (do not re-litigate without new evidence)

- The shell-parsing guard has **no command-injection bypass** (fable adversarial review + empirical harness against the real `cc_transcript` parser: separators, redirects, quoting, `$'...'`, backslash-newline, homoglyphs, subshells all fail closed).
- `--remote 'ext::sh -c …'` git-transport RCE is **blocked** by `ensureRemote` (`internal/sync/sync.go`), which requires an already-configured remote name before any fetch/push.
- The codex security-review lane hard-failed on an OpenAI cyber-content safety gate (the adversarial payload tripped it); the review was escalated to fable per the house rule.
ops.when: when adding a new cc-notes command or MCP tool, adjusting the permission carve-out, or reconsidering the approval model
